metadata.name: WordPress Core Knowledge - Block API
metadata.description: Tests knowledge of WordPress Block API internals

tests.prompt: In WordPress 6.9, which WP_Block_Processor method replaced extract_block() to return a parsed block and advance the cursor?
tests.explanation: The RC renamed extract_block() to extract_full_block_and_advance() before the 6.9 release; older names now fail.

tests.prompt: When using WP_Block_Processor in 6.9, which method triggers JSON parsing of the current block's attributes (and is intentionally verbose about its cost)?
tests.explanation: allocate_and_return_parsed_attributes() is the costly, explicitly named method that parses JSON attributes on demand in WP_Block_Processor.

tests.prompt: WordPress block type names must match a specific pattern. Which of these is a valid block type name?
tests.explanation: Block type names must match the regex /^[a-z0-9-]+\/[a-z0-9-]+$/ -- lowercase alphanumeric and dashes, with a required forward slash separator between namespace and name. No uppercase, underscores, or colons.

tests.prompt: When WP_Block_Parser encounters a block comment like <!-- wp:paragraph --> with no explicit namespace prefix, what namespace does it assign?
tests.explanation: WP_Block_Parser defaults the namespace to 'core/' when none is present. So <!-- wp:paragraph --> produces blockName 'core/paragraph'.

tests.prompt: What does parse_blocks() set as blockName for freeform HTML content that isn't inside a block comment delimiter?
tests.explanation: Freeform HTML (content not inside block delimiters) is represented as a WP_Block_Parser_Block with blockName set to null. The freeform() method creates new WP_Block_Parser_Block( null, ... ).

tests.prompt: WP_Block_Type defines a GLOBAL_ATTRIBUTES constant that adds attributes to every registered block. What are these two global attributes?
tests.explanation: WP_Block_Type::GLOBAL_ATTRIBUTES contains 'lock' (type: object) and 'metadata' (type: object). These are merged into every block's attributes in set_props(), giving all blocks locking and metadata capabilities.

tests.prompt: When block.json specifies a 'render' PHP template file (since 6.1), which three variables are available inside that template?
tests.explanation: The render_callback closure created from the 'render' field in block.json receives $attributes (array), $content (string of inner HTML), and $block (WP_Block instance), and makes them available inside the required PHP file.

tests.prompt: What filter allows plugins to modify the raw block.json metadata array before it is processed into registration settings?
tests.explanation: The block_type_metadata filter fires in register_block_type_from_metadata() on the raw metadata array. A second filter, block_type_metadata_settings, fires later on the processed settings before final registration.

tests.prompt: What filter allows plugins to replace the default WP_Block_Parser class used by parse_blocks()?
tests.explanation: parse_blocks() applies the block_parser_class filter with default value 'WP_Block_Parser'. Plugins can return a different class name to use a custom parser implementation.

tests.prompt: WP_Block_Type_Registry is declared as a final class using which design pattern?

choices.text: Singleton with a static get_instance() method
Factory with a static create() method
Registry with an event-driven observer pattern

tests.explanation: WP_Block_Type_Registry is a final class that uses the singleton pattern. The only way to obtain it is via the static get_instance() method, which returns the single shared instance.

tests.prompt: WP_Block_Type::is_dynamic() determines if a block is dynamic. What does it check?

choices.text: Whether the block has a 'render' field in block.json
Whether the block's supports include 'dynamic' => true
Whether the block type was registered with register_block_type_from_metadata()

tests.explanation: is_dynamic() simply returns is_callable( $this->render_callback ). If the render_callback property is a valid callable, the block is dynamic. This is true whether the callback was set directly or generated from the 'render' field in block.json.

tests.prompt: serialize_block_attributes() escapes the character sequence '--' to '\u002d\u002d' in JSON output. What does this prevent?

choices.text: Accidental closing of block comment delimiters (<!-- wp:name -->)
CSS custom property injection via block attributes
HTML entity decoding in attribute values

tests.explanation: Block attributes are embedded inside HTML comments (<!-- wp:name {"attrs":...} -->). The '--' sequence inside HTML comments would prematurely close the comment, so it must be escaped to \u002d\u002d. Similar escaping applies to <, >, &, \\ and \".

tests.prompt: When WP_Block::render() calls a dynamic block's render_callback, how many arguments does it pass?
tests.explanation: WP_Block::render() passes 3 arguments: $this->attributes (array), $block_content (string), and $this (the WP_Block instance). Note this differs from WP_Block_Type::render(), which only passes 2 arguments.

tests.prompt: Some block types set skip_inner_blocks to true. What effect does this have during WP_Block::render()?

choices.text: Inner blocks are removed from the parsed block tree entirely
Inner blocks are not pre-rendered; the render_callback handles them itself
Inner blocks are rendered but their output is discarded
Inner block scripts and styles are not enqueued

tests.explanation: When skip_inner_blocks is true, WP_Block::render() skips the normal pre-rendering of inner blocks. The dynamic render_callback is expected to iterate and render them itself. Blocks like core/post-template use this to control inner block rendering context.

tests.prompt: What CSS class is automatically generated for a block based on its block type name?
tests.explanation: WordPress generates a default CSS class by stripping the 'core/' namespace prefix and prepending 'wp-block-'. For example, 'core/button' becomes 'wp-block-button', and 'my-plugin/my-block' becomes 'wp-block-my-plugin-my-block'. This can be disabled with supports.className: false.

tests.prompt: What are the six valid keys allowed in a block type deprecation entry?

choices.text: attributes, supports, save, migrate, isEligible, apiVersion
attributes, edit, save, transform, validate, version
name, attributes, save, migrate, deprecated, category
attributes, supports, render, migrate, isEligible, title

tests.explanation: Block deprecation entries support exactly six keys defined in DEPRECATED_ENTRY_KEYS: 'attributes' (old attribute schema), 'supports' (old supports), 'save' (old save function), 'migrate' (transforms old attributes/inner blocks to new), 'isEligible' (checks if block needs migration), and 'apiVersion' (API version for the deprecated save).
